content.problemStatement:
In this example, we demonstrate how to simulate the progression of days in a week using a loop in C++. The program starts with a given day, then proceeds to the next day in the sequence using an `if-else` chain for each day.

You can define the starting day, and the loop will update the day for each iteration, printing the current day and the next day at each step. This allows you to track the progression from one day to the next, simulating the full weekly cycle.

**When executed, the code will display:**

```default
Starting day progression...
Current day: Monday
Next day will be: Tuesday
Next day will be: Wednesday
Next day will be: Thursday
Next day will be: Friday
Next day will be: Saturday
Next day will be: Sunday
Week progression completed!
```

This example illustrates how you can use a loop and conditional statements to simulate the flow of time (days in this case), displaying intermediate results and making it easy to understand how the program advances from one day to the next.
